rain_policy: Stadium has retractable roof — matches continue in all weather. Roof can close in 8 minutes.
heat_safety: Stay hydrated. Free water stations at every concourse level. Cooling mist zones near Gate A and Gate C.
sun_protection: Sunscreen recommended even with retractable roof. UV exposure during open-roof periods.
tips: Bring a reusable water bottle (empty at security, fill inside)
Take breaks in air-conditioned concourse areas

rain_policy: No roof. Matches continue in rain. Bring rain gear. Poncho recommended (umbrellas may block views).
heat_safety: Mild temperatures expected. Still bring water.
tips: Pack a light rain jacket or poncho
Layered clothing recommended — evenings can be cool
Seattle summers are generally pleasant but rain is always possible

rain_policy: Retractable roof closes in bad weather. All-weather stadium.
heat_safety: Extreme heat expected. Use cooling zones and stay hydrated. Medical stations available for heat-related illness.
sun_protection: High UV index. Sunscreen SPF 50+ recommended.
tips: Drink water frequently — don't wait until you're thirsty
Take advantage of air-conditioned concourse areas
Recognize heat exhaustion signs: dizziness, nausea, headache

rain_policy: No roof. Matches continue in rain. Ponchos recommended.
heat_safety: Stay hydrated. Free water stations available.
tips: Bring a hat and sunscreen for daytime matches
Air-conditioned areas available on Level 2 concourse

rain_policy: Retractable roof provides full weather protection.
heat_safety: Extreme heat in Texas summer. Stadium is climate-controlled inside.
sun_protection: Critical outside the stadium. Wear hat and use SPF 50+.
tips: Outside temperatures can exceed 100°F — plan arrival to minimize outdoor exposure
Covered parking in Garage available for additional cost
Bring empty water bottle to fill inside

rain_policy: No roof. Afternoon thunderstorms common in June-July. Pack rain gear.
heat_safety: Altitude: 2,240m (7,350 ft). Altitude sickness possible. Stay hydrated and acclimatize.
sun_protection: UV intensity is higher at altitude. Use SPF 50+.
tips: Mexico City is at high altitude — drink extra water and avoid alcohol before the match
Afternoon rain is common — bring a poncho
Temperature drops in the evening — bring a light jacket
If you feel dizzy or short of breath, seek medical attention (altitude sickness)
